political.s: FEC data shows UnitedHealth donated $2.2M with 5500/17000 (32%) going to Republicans, indicating left lean at corporate level. However, 2024 PAC contributions totaled $4.5M and lobbying spending reached $7.5M in 2024, escalating to $9.9M in 2025 amid DOJ Medicare fraud investigations. Company maintains bipartisan lobbying efforts with Republican-aligned consultants.

charity.s: United Health Foundation committed $880M total since 1999, including $100M over 10 years for health equity. 2024 giving programs include $11M+ Empowering Health grants across multiple states focusing on food insecurity, mental health, and social determinants. Employee matching and volunteer programs enhance giving capacity.
charity.focus: Health equity, affordable housing ($800M+ since 2011), food insecurity, mental health, healthcare workforce

environment.s: UnitedHealth committed to net-zero emissions by 2035 with 60% reduction in scope 1-2 emissions by 2030 and 100% renewable electricity by 2030. 4.3 billion pieces of paper eliminated since 2022 through digitalization. Closed 575M healthcare gaps since 2021, linking preventive care to environmental footprint reduction.

labor.s: Department of Labor recovered $934,551 in overtime back wages for 479 UnitedHealthcare employees in 2011 for misclassification violations. Additional FLSA overtime violation lawsuits filed in 2020. Violation Tracker shows $2.7B in penalties since 2000 across 449 records. Multiple claims of underpayment and employee misclassification.

dei.s: Company removed DEI content from website in March 2025 amid Trump administration pressure, replacing 'Diversity, Equity, Inclusion' pages with 'Culture of Belonging' with reduced content. Prior reports showed 45% people of color in workforce and 31% in management (2022). Current stance appears to be retreat from explicit DEI programs.

animals.s: Not applicable - healthcare insurance and services company with no animal testing operations.

guns.s: Not applicable - healthcare insurance and services company with no firearms sales or manufacturing.

privacy.s: UnitedHealth maintains standard HIPAA compliance and privacy policies for health information. However, company experienced major 2024 Change Healthcare ransomware breach exposing patient data. Privacy policies allow data sharing with vendors and third parties. Electronic communications may be sent unencrypted with acknowledged interception risk.

execPay.s: CEO Stephen Hemsley received $60.94M in 2025 (including $60M one-time equity award), with previous CEO Andrew Witty earning $26.3M in 2024. Median employee salary context shows executive compensation vastly exceeds worker pay. Company faced shareholder criticism over executive pay amid financial underperformance.

fec.committeeName: UNITEDHEALTH GROUP INCORPORATED PAC (UNITEDHEALTH GROUP PAC)

sec.note: Pay ratio extracted from proxy filing — verify manually for accuracy
